hero_product_name: the AnyWell powered wheelchair from the white-background reference photos
campaign_goal: Create three short anonymous vertical brand-film ads for Western markets inspired only by the emotional theme of quiet outdoor freedom near home.
target_market: United States, Canada, United Kingdom, and Western Europe
target_audience: Older adults, spouses, and adult children evaluating thoughtful mobility support for outdoor moments close to home.
core_selling_points:
- smooth starts
- steady control
- calm outdoor confidence
- more shared moments outside
use_scenarios:
- home doorway to porch
- finished ramp exit
- backyard path or sunset lane
visual_style: Warm, cinematic, realistic, portrait-first, restrained, mature Western brand advertising.
style_tone: warm, credible, calm, cinematic, never melodramatic
consistency_anchor: Match the real AnyWell wheelchair shown in the white-background product photos, but present it only in its normal open riding position. Preserve the front and side riding silhouette, right-side joystick, armrests, seat cushion scale, side housing, front caster, large drive wheel profile, tubular frame, footrest shape, and two tall black rear caregiver push handles. The push handles are distinct left and right vertical tubes rising clearly above the backrest, each ending in a short rearward-curved black rubber grip. Do not show any rear or lower external battery box, exposed battery cable, folded chair, semi-folded chair, compact storage form, or rear-facing product angle.
additional_info: Use the attached Jean source clip only as emotional direction: a quiet home edge, a warm spouse nearby, and the feeling of getting back outside. Do not recreate a real person, a specific home, the exact woods, a red porch chair, a white railing, an overhead porch camera angle, or an unfinished ramp. Every scene must feel newly generated, anonymous, and suitable for paid social testing.
music: Warm restrained cinematic underscore with light piano and gentle ambient texture.

concepts.title: From The Porch To The Trees
concepts.core_theme: Outdoor freedom can begin a few steps from home.
concepts.main_theme: Fresh air should still feel close.
concepts.hook: Fresh air should still feel close.
concepts.cta: See how AnyWell can support more quiet moments outdoors.
concepts.visual_style: Soft morning realism, generic backyard edge, calm handheld polish.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the same anonymous white-haired older man and the same spouse across both shots. Preserve wardrobe, hair, and wheelchair identity consistently.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- back doorway
- porch edge
- tree-lined backyard path
concepts.additional_info: This concept should feel inspired by the emotional shape of the source clip without matching its house, porch, or woods.

shots.scene_description: An anonymous white-haired older man sits in the AnyWell powered wheelchair at the back doorway of a generic North American home, ready to move outside. His right hand rests visibly on the right-side joystick controller. The camera stays on the front-side joystick angle so the viewer sees his front torso or soft facial profile, right forearm, joystick hand, armrest, front caster, and the two tall black rear caregiver push handles rising clearly above the backrest. His spouse stands nearby with a warm relaxed expression, not touching the chair. The home exterior is generic and anonymous, not blue siding, not a white railing, and not the exact source location.
shots.camera_movement: slow front-side push from doorway toward daylight, calm and steady
shots.lighting: soft morning natural light with clean outdoor highlights
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait front-side composition, generic home details, no readable address or unique decor
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair rolling forward toward a simple outdoor path
shots.voiceover_en: Fresh air should still feel close.
shots.key_message: The outdoors can begin right at home.
shots.sfx: soft tire roll and light morning birds

shots.scene_description: The same older man glides along a generic tree-lined backyard path in the same AnyWell powered wheelchair, self-operating with his right hand visibly on the right-side joystick controller. His spouse follows a step behind and to the side, smiling warmly without touching the chair. The camera tracks from the front-side joystick angle so the rider's chest or soft facial profile stays visible, along with the joystick hand, front-side wheel profile, and the two tall black rear caregiver push handles above the backrest. The setting feels calm and newly generated, not the exact bare-woods composition from the source clip.
shots.camera_movement: smooth front-side tracking shot with subtle forward drift
shots.lighting: filtered daylight through trees with realistic contrast
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium frame, generic path and trees, companion lightly in the background
shots.transition_anchor: end on steady forward motion into open air
shots.voiceover_en: A calm ride can open the outdoors again.
shots.key_message: Quiet confidence makes outdoor moments feel reachable.
shots.sfx: light wheel movement, leaves, and soft outdoor ambience

concepts.title: A Gentle Ramp Out
concepts.core_theme: A smoother start changes the whole feeling of the day.
concepts.cta: Discover a steadier start with AnyWell.
concepts.visual_style: Clean daytime realism, finished ramp, calm suburban exterior.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the same anonymous white-haired older man and the same spouse across both shots. Preserve one generic home exterior and one finished ramp.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- front threshold
- finished wooden ramp
- yard edge
concepts.additional_info: Use a finished ramp and generic home details. Do not recreate an unfinished ramp or the specific source porch.

shots.scene_description: The same anonymous older man pauses at the threshold of a generic home and begins a smooth move toward a finished wooden ramp in the AnyWell powered wheelchair. His right hand is visibly on the right-side joystick controller. The spouse stands close by with relaxed body language, not assisting physically. The camera stays on the front-side joystick angle, showing the rider's front torso or soft facial profile, the joystick hand, the front caster, and the two tall black rear caregiver push handles above the backrest. The environment feels clean and anonymous, with no direct match to the real source home.
shots.camera_movement: slow front-side move that follows the start toward the ramp
shots.lighting: bright natural daylight with soft warm reflections from wood surfaces
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait composition with ramp lines leading outward, generic suburban details, no unique identifiers
shots.transition_anchor: end as the chair settles into the ramp descent
shots.voiceover_en: A smoother start can change the whole day.
shots.sfx: soft porch ambience and subtle wheel start

shots.scene_description: The same older man reaches level ground and makes a gentle turn toward a yard-side path, still self-operating with his right hand visibly on the right-side joystick controller. The spouse walks nearby with hands free. The camera arcs around the front quarter of the chair so the rider's front torso or soft facial profile remains visible, along with the joystick hand and the two tall black rear caregiver push handles above the backrest. The scene emphasizes steady control and emotional ease instead of product-demo spectacle.
shots.camera_movement: smooth front-quarter arc that settles into a calm hero frame
shots.lighting: clear daylight with mild highlights and realistic shadows
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium-wide frame, finished ramp behind them, open yard ahead, rear lower chair area hidden
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair facing open space and slowing naturally
shots.voiceover_en: Steady control helps home lead back outside.
shots.key_message: A gentle exit can restore the feeling of possibility.
shots.sfx: quiet wheel roll, light breeze, and yard ambience

concepts.title: One More Evening Out
concepts.core_theme: Companionship is about sharing the light again.
concepts.main_theme: Some evenings are worth making room for.
concepts.hook: Some evenings are worth making room for.
concepts.cta: Explore more thoughtful outdoor moments with AnyWell.
concepts.visual_style: Golden-hour neighborhood edge, intimate but restrained, warm realism.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the same anonymous white-haired older man and the same spouse across both shots. Preserve the same wardrobe and evening light.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- driveway exit
- tree-lined neighborhood path
- sunset edge
concepts.additional_info: This concept should feel warm and quiet, never sentimental or testimonial-like.

shots.scene_description: At golden hour, the same anonymous older man sits just outside a generic home in the AnyWell powered wheelchair, ready to head toward the evening light. His right hand rests visibly on the right-side joystick controller. The spouse steps out beside him. The camera stays on the front-side joystick angle, keeping the rider's front torso or soft facial profile visible along with the joystick hand and the two tall black rear caregiver push handles above the backrest. The house and driveway remain generic and unidentifiable.
shots.camera_movement: slow drifting front-side push into warm evening light
shots.lighting: golden-hour sunlight with soft bloom and long natural shadows
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait composition, warm porch and driveway tones, no readable address or exact source details
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair moving gently toward the path
shots.voiceover_en: Some evenings are worth making room for.
shots.sfx: soft evening ambience and distant birds

shots.scene_description: The same couple moves along a generic tree-lined path under warm sunset light. The older man leads in the AnyWell powered wheelchair with his right hand visibly on the right-side joystick controller while the spouse walks beside him, matching pace without touching the chair. The camera tracks from the joystick-side front profile so the rider's front torso or soft facial profile remains visible, along with the joystick hand and the two tall black rear caregiver push handles above the backrest. The scene feels calm, dignified, and newly generated, not a testimonial re-creation.
shots.camera_movement: smooth front-profile tracking shot with subtle parallax from trees and sky
shots.lighting: warm low-angle sunset light with realistic contrast and soft edge highlights
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium-wide frame with path lines flowing toward a brighter horizon, soft non-identifiable faces
shots.transition_anchor: end on a quiet forward glide suitable for an end card
shots.voiceover_en: More than mobility. A quiet way to be there together.
shots.key_message: The promise is shared time outside.
shots.sfx: light wheel roll, evening breeze, and soft ambient fade
